message.content: I'm hitting an AttributeError in the text processing pipeline: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'split'. It only happens on some inputs, not all of them, and the traceback points into my normalize_text helper. Can you help me figure out what's going on here? I've been staring at it for a while and I'm not seeing the path that produces a None.

content.text: Let me read the helper and trace how it can return None.

message.content: Yes, normalize_text calls load_file first and then splits the result. I think load_file might be returning None when the file is empty or missing. Here is the snippet: when the path doesn't exist it logs a warning and falls through to the end of the function without an explicit return, so Python hands back None implicitly. Then the caller does text.split() on that None and blows up. Does that match what you're seeing?

content.text: Exactly — the implicit None on the missing-file branch is the culprit. Add a guard so callers get an empty list instead.

message.content: That fixed it. I added the guard `if text is None: return []` at the top of normalize_text and now the empty-file case returns an empty list instead of crashing. Thanks for walking through it — the implicit-None-from-missing-return was exactly the kind of thing I kept glossing over while reading the code. For the record, the failure mode was that load_file logged a warning on the missing-file branch and then fell off the end of the function, so Python returned None implicitly, and the caller immediately called .split() on it. I'm going to add a regression test that feeds normalize_text a path that doesn't exist and asserts it returns an empty list rather than raising, so this exact crash can't sneak back in during a future refactor of the loader.
